description:
Goal
Remove unresolved-as-`any` behavior from call, protocol, provider-boundary, and runtime-module wait paths. If a required module/interface/dispatch/return fact is missing, the job waits; if a boundary is intentionally dynamic, the evidence says so.

Signal
- `resolve_function_call`, `resolve_protocol_call`, and closure-call paths no longer return `any` for missing module definition, missing protocol dispatch, missing impl owner, missing function surface, or missing callee return evidence.
- Provider-boundary calls produce explicit dynamic evidence only when their contract/source says the boundary is dynamic or lacks a tighter return; this does not leak into local functions.
- Tests cover unresolved runtime module, protocol dispatch pending, missing impl owner, and dynamic provider return as distinct cases.
- Telemetry for Enum/protocol fixtures shows waits and evidence publication, not fake top return churn.

Tactics
- Work in `src/compiler2/jobs/semantic.rs` around call resolution and protocol dispatch.
- Reuse ReturnEvidence readiness instead of inventing a second missing-call state.
- If a provider contract genuinely returns `any`, keep that as explicit source semantics and name the test accordingly.

Outro
No unresolved function/module/protocol as `any`, no provider-boundary fallback that masks missing facts, no callsite summary published from absent evidence, and no runtime-helper special case.
title: Compiler2 calls: remove unresolved-as-any wait fallbacks
